questions.question: Which Software Engineering concept best matches: structured process from requirements to maintenance?
questions.explanation: Software Development Life Cycle is defined as: structured process from requirements to maintenance.

questions.question: What is the most accurate description of 'Software Development Life Cycle'?
options: testing functionality without internal code knowledge
percentage of code executed by tests
structured process from requirements to maintenance
statement describing what system must do
questions.explanation: Software Development Life Cycle refers to: structured process from requirements to maintenance.

questions.question: 'Software Development Life Cycle' belongs mainly to which software engineering area?
questions.explanation: Software Development Life Cycle is mainly part of SDLC Models.

questions.question: Identify the correct software engineering term-definition mapping.
options: Software Development Life Cycle -> structured process from requirements to maintenance
Modularity -> structured process from requirements to maintenance
Acceptance Testing -> structured process from requirements to maintenance
Equivalence Partitioning -> structured process from requirements to maintenance
questions.explanation: Only 'Software Development Life Cycle -> structured process from requirements to maintenance' is correctly mapped.

questions.question: A project requirement states: structured process from requirements to maintenance. Which concept should be selected?
questions.explanation: The requirement directly points to Software Development Life Cycle.
